Table 3. Phenotypic correlation between pairs of environments under two simulation approaches 529 (Sim1 and Sim2) and different levels of GEI. Values represent mean ± SD across 50 replicates. 530 GEI variance indicates the simulated magnitude of GEI effects (0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0). 531

Table 2. Monthly records of temperature at 2 meters (T2M, ℃) from four locations in 2023 used 524 in this study. Data were obtained from NASAPOWER database. E1 and E2 shared similar 525 environmental conditions, whereas E3 and E4 exhibited a different but internally consistent 526 environmental trend. 527
Table 6. The Euclidean distance between a pair of environments under two simulation 551 approaches (Sim1 and Sim2) in biplot. Values represent mean ± SD across 50 replicates. 552

Table 1. Parameters used for simulation of the recent population in this study. To assess the 518 effect of genotype × environment interaction (GEI) on phenotypic variation, four levels of GEI 519 variance (0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0) were applied across simulation replicates. Other simulation 520 parameters, including the number of markers, number of causal alleles, chromosome length, 521 population structure, mutation rate, and environmental/residual variance, were set as indicated. 522
Table 5. The Pearson’s correlations between observed phenotypes and predicted values from 543 three models. Values represent mean ± SE across 50 replicates. 544
